Module #3
With Dan Kennedy

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 4
M ODULE #3
Dan Kennedy: The media I want almost every business to use for Magnetic
Marketing is their own direct mail.
In just about any and every case I can think of, I would use direct mail and I would
use the three-step letter sequences before I would use anything else. I would
concentrate my resources there.
This is the most controllable, trackable, changeable tool that you have.
You can do a mailing this week, you can know what the results are next week.
• 	If they were good, you may want to increase that mailing and do it again
the following week.
• 	If they weren't so good, you can change one variable, change two variables
and do another small unit test.
You can test these kinds of letters for local businesses in fairly small quantities,
200 units, 300 units, and get a feel for how well
they're going to work.
There's just about no other method of localized target
marketing that you can test for as little money and get
the refinement done for as little money.
As I go into this example, understand that direct mail
is not the only thing I teach, not the only thing you
should use and not the only thing in my system.
In fact, far from it, but to be truly in control of your
own destiny you want to own a stable of reliable
direct mail campaigns based on the three-step model.
As an example, a financial advisor I work with in Pennsylvania has…
To be truly in
control of your own
destiny you want a
stable of reliable
direct mail
campaigns based on
the three-step
model.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 5
• 	A three-step campaign that fills his introductory workshops
• 	A three-step campaign to generate leads requesting a free report
• 	A three-step follow-up campaign to people who attend the workshops,
but fail to immediately book appointments
These are all used consistently seven to ten times a year. These have fueled his
million-dollar a year business with nominal changes for at least five years.
A dentist with four offices has three-step letter campaigns built right out of the
Magnetic Marketing system for…
• 	New movers
• 	Geodemographic neighborhoods
• 	Leads obtained initially from online and social media
• 	Appointment no sale – when he makes a case presentation to a patient and
does not get immediate acceptance
• 	Lost inactive patients
These are responsible for about 50% of his practice's revenue and they have been
locked down and used with virtually no changes for almost seven years.
It is nearly impossible to get that kind of long term use out of advertising done in
online, print, or broadcast media.
E XPERIENCE OF D IRECT M AIL
Still, as soon as I start to talk about doing your own direct mail, I hear, "We've
tried direct mail. It doesn't work in our state, city, business, industry, product
category. It's too expensive. It's too complicated."
A couple of things about that. First of all, if you and I go through life only doing
the things that work real, real well for us the very first time we do them, there
would be less kids with each generation.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 6
Second, you have not tried direct mail the way I am going to show you how to do
direct mail.
In fact, almost everybody makes this mistake when they do try it. They get a list
from somewhere, maybe not a very good list, but they get a list, they print up
something, they send it to the list and almost no matter what happens, ugly, bad,
good, they never mail to those same people again.
Or, if they do, it's three, six, eight months later by accident and they send them
the same stuff they sent them the first time.
Save your money. Never do one-shot mail. Let me explain to you why that can't
work.
Here's how people do not live. The last thing Harvey says to Marge when he leaves
the house is not…
"Marge, I want you to stay home. Don't go to work. Don't take the kids to school.
Don't go to the grocery store. Do not leave this house. Stay right here in the foyer
with your nose pressed against the window watching for the mailman.
When the mailman arrives, I want you to hustle your
buns out to the mailbox and get the mail before
somebody gets it, a bird snatches it, it catches on fire or
it gets wet. Get it back in the house. Guard it until I get
home.
Together, we will open the mail because today might be the day that a life insurance
agent sends us one of those nifty letters where we can get a free Starbucks gift card
if we let him come over to our house and beat our brains in for three hours and I
want that Starbucks card."
This is not how people live their lives.
Save your money.
Never do one-shot
mail.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 7
What makes people think they can print up one crummy goldenrod flyer or a big
postcard, send it out one time and get some stampede of response? It's a mystery
to me.
The thing doesn't even nick them on the way by. They don't even feel the breeze.
The example I am going to cover here is a different thing entirely, so pay close
attention because there are a lot of lessons here that you can apply not only to
direct mail, but to other forms of media as well.
T HE G IORGIO L ETTERS
The example I am going to show you comes from the restaurant industry.
I use it for two reasons. One, if you had a room full of restaurant owners, they'd
all swear it won't work. If they do mail or use Valpak, they do simple basic
lookalike offers, typically discount and cheap price driven, “Two dinners for one
price with free dessert.” That's their only idea of a marketing message.
This example shows how you can take your marketing message, deliver it to a
chosen market effectively, efficiently and affordably and magnetically bring ready
to buy customers and clients and dominate a market in 45 days or less.
In a controlled test 25 years ago, from this campaign mailed to 2,000 households,
a restaurant booked about 140 dinners.
Since then literally thousands of restaurants have used this mailing or one
adapted from it and enjoyed 2%, 5%, even 7% response acquiring new first-time
customers.
I doubt seriously if you could find any other approach to advertising and
marketing that would get those kinds of results for restaurants.
You can look at the Giorgio letters in your toolkit.
Essentially, I invented a new business within the business. I hope that you can see
that.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 8
I invented the romance business and plugged it in to the restaurant business and
dramatically changed the marketing message.
Letter number one goes to a geo-
demographically selected list for an
Italian restaurant. In real life, where
the square is, there's a photograph of
the owner of the restaurant.
The 	headline 	says, 	“A 	Confidential
Letter to the Husband of the House,
From Giorgio, the Romance Director of
Giorgio's Italian Grotto.”
I'll read you just two of the paragraphs:
“Dear Husband, Women are different
than we are. Your loving wife needs,
wants and deserves special attention
maybe more often than you think to give it to her.
You are busy, preoccupied with work, aggravated with that dumb-dumb you have
to deal with every day at the office. Tired. Who has the time or the energy to even
think about romance?
Two thirds of all marriages end in divorce and the number one reason given by
divorcing women, “He just didn't pay enough attention to me anymore.’”
I wrote this while I was watching Oprah. You do remember Oprah, don't you?
It goes on to present a solution to the problem, which in this case is a prepackaged
evening of romance – table in a special section, five course meal, strolling violinist,
rose in a bud vase, heart shaped box of candy to take home, souvenir photograph,
all for one set price.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 9
That's the widget that was created for
this sequence.
What happens to everybody that gets
this letter and does not respond? 15
days later, they get a second one.
This one, too, has a picture of Giorgio
in real life. Where the circles are, there
are three pennies glued to the letter.
The headline says: “Three coins in the
fountain.”
The second paragraph says:
“You see, this is your second notice, your
romance wake-up call from me, Giorgio
the romance director. My bell tolls. Does it toll for thee?”
This letter goes on to restate the problem, restate the solution, remake the offer.
It works and gets response.
What happens to everybody that gets
letter number one, letter number two,
who still does not respond?
Ten days later, they get letter number
three.
Letter number three has a picture of
Giorgio.
The headline says: “Hear that lonesome whippoorwill? He sounds too blue to cry.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 10
“Dear Husband, this is Giorgio, too blue to cry, disappointed. Attached are copies of
the two letters I previously sent you.”
The real stubborn ones that don't respond to that soon get a postcard that says:
“Can this marriage be saved?”
Do you have any doubt that in any household that gets the three letters, and if
necessary, the postcard, Giorgio is not the topic of conversation?
It doesn't matter who opens the mail. They're showing it to each other. They're
showing it to their neighbors. “Are you getting this guy’s mail?” Some are and
some aren't.
When Giorgio walks into a 7-11 or into a dry cleaners in the community, people
gather around, tell him how much they enjoy getting his mail, when they've got
reservations at the restaurant and ask for his autograph.
For the price of three letters and a postcard, he is the dominant presence in his
category of business in 45 days or less in his chosen target market.
You can't do it more efficiently than that.
T HE R IGHT M ARKET
Now a few people are saying, “Wait a minute. I sell
very sophisticated stuff to very sophisticated people
and 	I 	could 	never 	do 	anything 	like 	that. 	It's
unprofessional.”
You can separate style from structure, but I want you
to know this works even better in business to business than it does in consumer.
Everything else they get in business to business mail is deadly dull, institutional
and boring.
I've used variations on Giorgio in marketing to hospital CEOs, financial advisors
and doctors, keeping the tongue in cheek style and tone.
This works even
better in business to
business than it does
in consumer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 11
T HE H ISTORY
I came up with the structure many years ago where in one year I managed to have
two cars repossessed and go personally and corporately bankrupt. I got it all over
with at once.
During that year, I became intimately familiar with the collection industry and I
noticed a pattern – first notice, second notice, third notice, 15 days apart.
There's no mystery. They're writing to you repeatedly. It's technically called
'Linkage'. Each letter refers to the previous letter.
Generally, the last one has copies of everything they sent you before with 'Final
Notice' rubber stamped all over it, stuffed in the envelope.
I saw it over and over and over again. I said, “If this will get money from people
who haven't got any, offering them nothing, I wonder what would happen if we
tried it on people who've got some money and offered
them something.”
It 	became 	one 	of 	my 	most 	reliable 	Magnetic
Marketing models and I commend it to you highly for
you to try and to apply some determination and
persistence to make it work.
Owning 	a 	campaign 	like 	this 	that 	you 	can 	use
repeatedly over a long period of time to acquire customers is a form of business
and financial security as good as, and maybe even better than money in the bank.
A lot of business owners balk at all of this because they are intimidated by the
idea of writing sales letters and ads that work like the ones we've been talking
about.
I want to give you some methods that will make it easier to do than you might
think.
Owning a campaign
like this to acquire
customers may be
even better than
money in the bank.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 12
M AKING IT E ASY
The first suggestion is, maybe obviously, use the examples in your toolkit.
Borrow, adapt, but stay as true to them as you can.
Make your starting point the big idea of your most desirable benefit and instantly
communicate that. Usually, this is going to impact your very first up front
headline.
Headlines and all marketing really revolve around some very basic motives and
some very basic techniques.
There's a classic headline format that says, “They Didn't Think I Could [fill in the
blank] But I Did.”
That comes from a famous ad written by John Caples who is a legend in the
advertising industry. There's even an annual Caples Award for copywriting. The
ad sold millions of dollars of home study piano lessons and its headline said,
“They Laughed When I Sat Down at The Piano… Until I Started to Play.”
This uses something that is very desirable to all of us, the desire to impress the
people that we're around, to be an important person. We all want to show off.
At parties, at work, we want to tell the best joke. We want to have the newest joke.
We want to be able to do the trick. We want to be able to pick all the football
games right on Friday so that on Monday we can gloat about it with our co-
workers. That kind of thing turns us all on.
A headline that says, “Everybody Laughed at Me. Nobody Thought I Could Do This,
and Then I Then I Stuck it to Them and I Did It,” that gets folks excited. You can
apply it to just about anything.
It was also used for another home study company selling foreign language
courses. “They Grinned When the Waiter Spoke to Me in French, But Their
Laughter Changed to Amazement at My Reply.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 13
With this, you're pushing emotional buttons. That, in and of itself, is a very
important point.
E MOTIONAL S ELL
Many business owners believe that their business or their product is not subject
to an emotional sell.
They'll tell me, “That works fine for some kind of diet plan you're going to sell in
the National Enquirer, but my business has nothing to do with emotion.”
All businesses have to do with emotion, because the only way anybody ever
makes a decision to respond to anything is on an emotional level.
Then we apply logic to make ourselves feel good about what we decided to do in
the first place, but we respond first emotionally.
If you want to get favorable attention, you have to operate using emotional
buttons.
One path to a big idea and a great headline is to figure
out the emotional payoff of the benefits of your
products or services.
There are always functionality benefits and always
emotional benefits.
New dentures provide functionality benefits. You can
eat, chew, enjoy eating and have better health, but the
emotional payoff of “Never being embarrassed about smiling again” is more
magnetic.
To a business owner, having a well-trained staff has functionality benefits, but the
emotional payoff of pride, being constantly complimented about your staff, is
more magnetic.
The only way
anybody ever makes
a decision to
respond to anything
is on an emotional
level.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 14
S ELLING S ECRETS
Another headline that has broad application and has its own emotional payoff
built in is, “Secrets of [blank] Revealed.”
If you want to jazz it up further you stick a number in front of it: “49 Secrets,” “22
Secrets,” “72 Secrets,” or you make it “The Secret Somebody Doesn't Want You to
Know.”
This works because we all, deep down inside believe that there are secrets, that
somebody knows some secrets that we don't know, and if we just knew them, that
would make the difference.
There is also some ego and pride in knowing things other people do not know.
That's one of the ways that this system is being sold to you, on the premise that
there are marketing secrets, and it becomes a true premise if I know something
that you don't know. To you, it is a secret.
A very famous headline is simply, “Secrets of Four Champion Golfers Revealed.”
Golfers are the most manic people on earth about improving their golf games.
Golfers will do anything. They will hike barefoot to the top of the mountains of
Tibet to find the wise golfer who will tell them how to shave one stroke off their
score. They will do anything to improve their golf games.
If a headline says, “Secrets of Four Champion Golfers,” they want to know what
those secrets are.
How could a local business use that headline? Any sporting goods store could use
it. Any country club could use it.
Could a restaurant use that headline? Sure, a restaurant could host Golfer Day and
invite the golf pros from the local country club to be there free to promote their
business.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 15
They would give a little half hour lunchtime golf instruction seminar out in the
parking lot. The restaurant runs it as a promotion to attract customers to their
restaurant.
That headline, “Secrets of ....” can apply to just about anything. Certainly you can
use that headline in the beauty business. “Secrets of Younger Looking Skin.
Secrets Hollywood Actresses Know for Great Hair.”
P ROBLEMS S OLVED
Another kind of headline starts with a hated problem you solved instead of with
a big benefit you offer.
A successful example is, “Do You Hate Your Computer?” The copy that follows is,
“Do you own a computer that won't do what you tell it to do? Are you afraid to buy
a new computer even though you know you need one? Are you confused by computer
babble?”
That headline is designed to cause immediate identification, an affinity if you will,
and we talked about affinity earlier, with anybody who opens that letter who does
have problems with their computer.
Another headline formula I call “Fortune Telling.” A really good prediction
headline, for example, for a company selling burglar alarms in a sales letter going
out to a neighborhood might be, “At Least One Home Within Walking Distance of
Yours Will Be Robbed This Year.”
A CID T EST FOR H EADLINES
I can give you an acid test for the headlines of most ads or sales letters. The test
is based on the National Enquirer, which, like it or not, has the widest, largest
circulation of any media. More people read the National Enquirer than read
anything else.
For decades, the Enquirer was home of some of the most profitable classified ads.
Print media classified advertising has been drained away by Craigslist, eBay and
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 16
other online media and the principles of print also migrate there, but a classified
ad is a good place to learn how to do this.
A classified ad is nothing more than a headline and a how-to instruction with all
the copy taken out of the middle.
Our test is to take the headline out of your ad, out of context, away from
everything else, and run it as a classified ad in the National Enquirer with nothing,
but its headline and a “how to respond” instruction and ask ourselves, 'Would
anybody respond?'
Let's take a headline you might see on a typical, local
small businesses Yellow Pages ad, Valpak coupon,
website and other media.
The headline is, “Sam's Bowling Alley.” That's the
headline.
We put it in the National Enquirer and our classified
ad reads like this:
“Sam's Bowling Alley, Box #3, Grand Central Station, New York, New York”
How many letters are you going to get? None.
There's no reason for anybody to write to you so the ad fails, the headline fails.
That's the National Enquirer test.
Instead, Sam might come up with a headline for his bowling alley ad:
“10 Secret Ways to Add 100 Points to Your Bowling Score”
If we run that in the National Enquirer with, “Write to Box #3, Grand Central
Station, New York, New York,” I promise you we're going to get some mail.
That's a great headline for a Facebook ad or a Valpak coupon.
A classified ad is
nothing more than a
headline and a how-
to instruction with
all the copy taken
out of the middle.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 17
C OPY F ORMULAS
Beyond the opening gambit, there are formulas for the full copy in copywriting of
an ad, a sales letter, a video sales letter placed online and other media.
There are very sure footed reliable easy to use formulas in my book, “The
Ultimate Sales Letter, Fourth Edition.”
There are formulas in another of my books, “The No B.S. Guide to Powerful
Presentations.”
Most of the examples in your Magnetic Marketing toolkit are built with these
formulas.
One of the most common and one of the easiest formulas to learn is called
“Problem, Agitate, and Solve.”
You setup the existence of a problem that your reader has and you get him
emotionally worked up about the effects of that problem on him, then you whip
out the answer to that problem that is going to solve
it instantly and quickly and easily for him.
When you learn the formulas, writing becomes easy.
You may think, “Geez, I have trouble writing a grocery
list that makes any sense right now,” but with these
formulas, you become a good sales writer.
You may not necessarily win a grammar award, you might not necessarily be able
to write a novel, but you will be able to write great letters that sell and that get
response just by following the formulas.
T AKING F INANCIAL C ONTROL
I'll wrap up by talking with you about money. What Magnetic Marketing is really
all about is financial control of your business and financial efficiency.
When you learn the
formulas, writing
becomes easy.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 18
It puts you more firmly in control of your attraction of new customers. It also
enables you to attract better customers for your particular business.
You have to understand that the customer is an asset to be acquired by
investment and to be taken care of and maintained and that you have an
opportunity to start with at value A, and build all the way to better and bigger
value B with each customer that you get and each customer that you have.
For that to happen, you have to do as good of a job at marketing to your existing
customers as you do at marketing to get new customers.
Just as you acquire them with Magnetic Marketing, you can develop them, retain
them, and sequentially sell to them with Magnetic Marketing.
One of the most important keys to maximum customer value is in retention and
repeat business. That is not an entitlement from excellent products and excellent
service. It is also a result of excellent marketing. That includes continuing to
market to them, not taking them for granted and communicating again and again
in a magnetic manner.
This requires frequently having and presenting a good answer to the question
that is so important to us that it's become part of our vernacular. It's how we greet
each other.
The question is, “What's new?” I don't say, “Tell me everything that's the same as
it was the last time I saw you three months ago.” I'm not interested in that. I'm
interested in “What's new.”
If you don't have a good answer to “What's new?” they go looking for it
somewhere else. A bored customer goes elsewhere. They forget about you. They
don't talk about you. They don't buy from you and they are easily wooed by some
competitor who comes along with something that's new and exciting.
How do you answer this question that is always on their minds? I've talked about
that answer here and it is shown to you in many of the Toolkit examples.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 19 --

M AGNETIC M ARKETING
© 2017 Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle, LLC. All rights reserved. 	Page 19
You answer this question by frequently and continually reinventing your
business inside your business, by inventing new widgets and offering those new
widgets through constant communication with your past and present customers.
At least once a month if not more often, they are hearing from you and they are
getting a new widget offered to them. That will keep customers, keep customers
interested and engaged, sell to them more frequently and even get them to refer
more 	often. 	Point 	is, 	your 	complete 	Magnetic 	Marketing 	system 	should
incorporate what, in direct marketing lingo, is called “Frontend and backend”
• 	Frontend refers to outreach to attract and acquire new leads, new
potential customers and new customers.
• 	Backend means developing and retaining those customers, increasing the
frequency of repeat business you do with them, cross selling different
products and services, putting fresh offers in front of them frequently,
staying interesting to them so they stay with you and tell others about you.
I hope what I've had to say here and what you learn and discover throughout the
Magnetic Marketing system motivates you to work on and develop your own
complete system.
I know that we have given you the tools and the strategies to do exactly that.
However, if you find that you need or want assistance with any of the ideas,
strategies or tools, or you'd like some hands-on assistance with some of your
marketing problems or business challenges, the team at GKIC welcomes your call
or email.
There are a variety of ways that you can get support, coaching, and even
customized 	implementation 	from 	GKIC, 	the 	undisputed 	leader 	in 	direct
marketing for all kinds of businesses. You have come to the right place.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 19 --
